answer: Standard Ecwid store plans require a paid subscription to access the REST API. You cannot access the API on a standard free tier. However, if you are a developer, Ecwid provides a free upgrade to a paid plan for your test store if you are building an integration, developing a public application, or working on a store customization project. To request this, you must contact their API support team via email. Once you have a store with access, you can manage your integration and generate access tokens through the application dashboard in your Ecwid admin panel.
